title: celtis koraiensis var. aurantiaca
intro: Celtis koraiensis var. aurantiaca, also known as Korean sweetgum, is a plant species in the family Celastraceae, native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing blood sugar levels, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing anti-cancer properties. Traditionally, Korean sweetgum is prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
